questions.question: What is the main benefit of using CSS image sprites?
questions.explanation: Image sprites combine multiple images into one file, reducing the number of HTTP requests needed to load a page.

questions.question: Which CSS property is used to display the correct portion of a sprite?
questions.explanation: background-position with negative values shifts the visible portion of the sprite background to reveal the desired icon.

questions.question: How do you specify a sprite icon that is the third item in a row of 50px icons?
questions.explanation: The third icon (index 2) starts at position: -(2 × 50px) = -100px. The first icon is at 0, second at -50px, third at -100px.

questions.question: What should you set background-repeat to when using sprites?
questions.explanation: background-repeat: no-repeat prevents the sprite from tiling and showing multiple icons within the element.

questions.question: Which modern CSS technique has reduced the need for image sprites?
questions.explanation: Icon fonts, inline SVGs, SVG sprites, and CSS gradients have all reduced the need for traditional image sprites in modern development.

questions.question: What must you set on an element to use a sprite as a background?
questions.explanation: The element must have explicit width and height matching one icon in the sprite. Without these, the sprite won't display correctly.

questions.question: For a sprite with 50x50px icons on a 2x Retina display, what background-size should you use?
questions.explanation: For Retina displays, use a double-resolution sprite with background-size: 50% so each icon maps to the correct physical pixel dimensions.

questions.question: How do you create a sprite sheet animation in CSS?
questions.explanation: Sprite animations use @keyframes to animate background-position with steps() timing, jumping between frames for a flip-book effect.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of SVG sprites over image sprites?
options: SVGs scale seamlessly at any resolution
questions.explanation: SVG sprites scale perfectly at any resolution and DPI, while image sprites need separate Retina versions.

questions.question: What happens if the single sprite image fails to load?
options: All icons using that sprite become invisible
The icons appear with alt text
questions.explanation: Since all icons depend on the same sprite file, if it fails to load, every icon using that sprite becomes invisible — a single point of failure.

questions.question: What does the background-position value 0 -50px do in a sprite?
options: Shows the icon in the second row, first column
Shows the icon at 50px from the top
Shows the second icon in the first row
questions.explanation: background-position: 0 -50px shifts the sprite up by 50px, revealing the icon in the second row (y-offset of one icon height) at the first column.

questions.question: In HTTP/2 environments, why might sprites be less necessary?
options: HTTP/2 supports multiplexing over a single connection
questions.explanation: HTTP/2 multiplexing allows multiple requests over a single connection, reducing the overhead that made sprites critical for HTTP/1.1 performance.

questions.question: How do you include hover states in a sprite sheet?
options: Use a separate sprite for hover
Include hover states in the same sprite, in a second row
questions.explanation: Hover states are typically placed in the second row of the sprite. The :hover pseudo-class changes background-position to the hover row.

questions.question: What is the recommended minimum number of images to justify using a sprite?
questions.explanation: Sprites are most beneficial when combining 5+ small images. For just 2-3 images, the overhead of sprite creation may not be worthwhile.

questions.question: What calculation determines the background-position for a 32x32px icon at row 3, column 4?
questions.explanation: Column 4 (index 3): -(3 × 32px) = -96px. Row 3 (index 2): -(2 × 32px) = -64px. So background-position: -96px -64px.

questions.question: How can you use sprites with ::before pseudo-elements for icon buttons?
options: Set the sprite as background-image on ::before with explicit dimensions
Use background on the element itself
questions.explanation: ::before pseudo-elements can display sprite icons by setting width, height, background-image, and background-position on the pseudo-element.

questions.question: What setting on a sprite animation @keyframes ensures smooth frame-by-frame playback?
questions.explanation: steps() timing function creates a frame-by-frame animation by jumping between discrete values, perfect for sprite sheet frame sequences.

questions.question: What accessibility concern applies to sprite-based icon buttons?
options: They need aria-label or screen-reader-only text to describe the action
Sprites can't be read by screen readers
Sprite buttons are not keyboard accessible
questions.explanation: Since sprites are background-images (decorative), interactive elements using sprites need aria-label or visually hidden text to convey their purpose.

questions.question: What file format is recommended for sprite sheet animations?
questions.explanation: WebP offers good compression for sprite animations while supporting transparency. PNG is also common but produces larger files.

questions.question: What tool is commonly used for automated sprite generation?
questions.explanation: Multiple build tools can automate sprite generation: webpack sprite plugins, Compass SCSS sprite mixins, and Gulp/Grunt plugins all handle sprite creation.
